phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine metabolic syndrome with benign lipomatous tumors and recurrent mild major depression. A separate cluster features hip pathology (avascular necrosis and femoral neck fracture), alongside acute myeloid leukemia and substance use disorders. These represent unrelated mechanisms and organ systems without a single unifying clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Lymphoid malignancies with Crohn disease
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects lymphoid malignancies—non-Hodgkin lymphoma subtypes (including diffuse large B‑cell, mantle cell, MALT, nodular forms) and chronic lymphocytic leukemia—with associated cytopenias (neutropenia, pancytopenia, thrombocytopenia), anemia, hypogammaglobulinemia, splenomegaly, and fever. Crohn disease and its intestinal complications co-occur, pointing to gastrointestinal immune involvement alongside systemic immune dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Type 1 diabetes with microalbuminuria
phenotypes.description: Predominant pattern is type 1 diabetes with hyperglycemia, dyslipidemia, and microalbuminuria. This indicates early diabetic kidney involvement and elevated cardiometabolic risk. Occasional asplenia/immunosuppression and gastrointestinal malignancy codes appear in a minority but are secondary to the diabetes phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Sickle cell and thalassemia with cardiomyopathy
phenotypes.description: Patients exhibit hemoglobinopathies centered on sickle cell disease (hemoglobin SS) and thalassemia, including crisis and non-crisis states. Iron metabolism disorders co-occur with cardiomyopathy and heart failure, consistent with transfusional iron overload–related cardiac involvement.

phenotypes.label: Heritable aortopathy with valvular disease
phenotypes.description: Features include aortic aneurysm, aortic root dilatation, congenital aortic insufficiency, and bicuspid aortic valve, with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome indicating a heritable connective tissue basis. Ocular changes and allergic or airway conditions may co-occur, but the dominant theme is heritable aortopathy.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix nonspecific abdominal symptoms with heterogeneous gynecologic and intra‑abdominal neoplasm terms, postoperative states, and general comorbidities. There is no single coherent clinical pattern tying these together, so this is not a usable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Diverticulitis, wrist fractures, thyroid
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions span unrelated systems: diverticulitis of the colon, distal radius/carpal fractures, glossodynia, and subclinical hyperthyroidism/thyrotoxicosis. These do not share a single clinical mechanism, reflecting a blend of gastrointestinal inflammation, traumatic wrist fractures, oral pain syndromes, and thyroid dysfunction in one cluster.

phenotypes.label: Colorectal and anal malignancies
phenotypes.description: Primary malignant neoplasms of the colon, rectum, rectosigmoid junction, anus, and anal canal, with associated anorectal disorders and bleeding. Findings such as hemorrhoids, anal fissure, fecal incontinence, diarrhea/constipation, and documented colostomy or ileostomy reflect anorectal symptomatology and postoperative anatomy in colorectal/anal cancer.

phenotypes.label: Wrist pain and ganglion cysts
phenotypes.description: Focal wrist disorders dominated by wrist joint pain, stiffness, and left-sided wrist pain, with ganglion cysts and tenosynovitis common. Represents localized musculoskeletal/overuse pathology of the wrist and hand.

phenotypes.label: CNS tumors and demyelinating disease
phenotypes.description: Central nervous system disorders featuring intracranial neoplasms (meningioma and primary brain tumors) and demyelinating disease (multiple sclerosis). Neuro‑ophthalmic manifestations such as optic neuritis, visual field defects, diplopia, and optic atrophy coexist with seizures, hydrocephalus, and cerebral edema, reflecting mass effect or inflammatory optic pathway involvement.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar disorder and PTSD
phenotypes.description: Dominated by bipolar spectrum disorders and posttraumatic stress disorder, with occasional severe major depression with psychotic features and schizoaffective bipolar type. Substance use comorbidity (alcohol/cocaine) appears alongside head and facial injuries (nasal fracture, abrasions, contusions), indicating trauma-associated psychiatric illness.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading concepts largely mirror broadly recorded breast cancer diagnoses and routine perioperative or imaging-related entries that are common across patients. This grouping does not isolate a distinct clinical pattern beyond the general background.

phenotypes.label: Head-neck cancer and spinal cord injury
phenotypes.description: This combines head and neck malignancies (larynx, oropharynx, tongue) with dysphagia, dysphonia, vocal cord disorders, and neck mass findings. It also includes spinal cord injury sequelae such as paraplegia and neurogenic bladder, which are unrelated to the same clinical picture.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis with complications
phenotypes.description: Inflammatory bowel disease dominated by ulcerative colitis, including pancolitis and proctitis, with complications such as rectal bleeding and chronic blood loss iron‑deficiency anemia. Many records reflect surgical sequelae (ileostomy, ileal pouchitis) and infectious or hepatobiliary associations including Clostridioides difficile colitis and primary sclerosing cholangitis.

phenotypes.label: Melanoma and chronic rhinosinusitis
phenotypes.description: Cutaneous malignancy is prominent, led by melanoma and other primary skin cancers with head-and-neck involvement. There is strong co-occurrence of chronic rhinosinusitis and nasal structural disease (deviated septum, turbinate hypertrophy, nasal polyps). Autoimmune dermatoses and connective‑tissue disorders (psoriasis, dermatomyositis, systemic sclerosis) and some soft‑tissue sarcomas also appear within this pattern.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer and parathyroid disorders
phenotypes.description: Endocrine profile centered on thyroid neoplasms (papillary thyroid carcinoma and other thyroid tumors), nodular/goiter disease, and autoimmune thyroiditis with Graves-related thyrotoxicosis, leading to hypo- or hyperthyroidism. Prominent postoperative hypothyroidism and hypoparathyroidism with hypocalcemia suggest thyroidectomy sequelae, with accompanying primary hyperparathyroidism, calcium disturbances, vitamin D deficiency, and osteoporosis reflecting parathyroid and bone metabolism involvement.

phenotypes.label: Onychomycosis with peripheral vascular disease
phenotypes.description: Cluster of podiatric nail pathology dominated by onychomycosis, nail dystrophy/onychogryphosis, and callus formation. It is associated with peripheral arterial disease and diabetes-related angiopathy/dermopathy, reflecting diabetic foot and ischemic changes of the nails and skin.

phenotypes.label: Dry eye, cataract, refractive errors
phenotypes.description: Age-related ophthalmic conditions predominate: presbyopia; myopia, hyperopia, and astigmatism; tear film insufficiency with blepharitis/meibomian gland dysfunction; ocular hypertension or glaucoma suspect; and nuclear senile cataract. Cutaneous sun-exposure lesions and ocular adnexal/uveal tumors appear alongside, including actinic keratoses, basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, and eyelid or choroidal neoplasms.

phenotypes.label: Hematologic neoplasms, pancreatic cancer, pain
phenotypes.description: This combines unrelated condition clusters: hematologic neoplasms (essential thrombocythemia, myelodysplastic syndromes, monoclonal gammopathy/Waldenström), pancreatic malignancy, and prominent musculoskeletal pain with somatic dysfunction codes, alongside depressive disorders. These represent different organ systems and mechanisms without a single unifying clinical story.

phenotypes.label: No distinct clinical theme
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are ubiquitous chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (e.g., hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, obesity, sleep apnea, chronic pain, anxiety, osteoarthritis). This reflects broad baseline medical history rather than a cohesive condition pattern and is not useful for defining a focused clinical cohort.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine syncope and cerebrovascular events with unrelated herpes simplex dermatitis, varicose veins, knee injuries, and mood/eating disorders. These represent disparate body systems without a unifying mechanism, so this is not a single clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy with peripartum mood disorders
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy and the peripartum period are central, with obstetric complications including preeclampsia and postpartum hemorrhage. Prominent postpartum mood and psychotic conditions appear, such as depression, mania/hypomania, and hallucinations, with visual symptoms consistent with hypertensive disease. This reflects peripartum obstetric events accompanied by significant maternal mental health disorders.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This bundle mixes very common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, depression/anxiety, obesity, tobacco use) with nonspecific pain and respiratory complaints. It reflects a general background health profile rather than a distinct clinical phenotype, with no single underlying condition linking the findings.

phenotypes.label: Bladder cancer with hematuria
phenotypes.description: Urologic pattern centered on bladder and ureteral malignancies accompanied by hematuria (gross and microscopic). Co-occurring findings include cystitis/UTI, dysuria, urinary retention, kidney stones, hydronephrosis, and ureteral stricture/occlusion. This reflects urothelial cancer patients presenting with bleeding and obstructive or irritative urinary symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Temporal arteritis and knee meniscus
phenotypes.description: Includes temporal arteritis with possible large‑vessel involvement (e.g., ascending aortic aneurysm) alongside medial meniscus derangement of the knee. These are unrelated conditions without a unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The top conditions reflect generic symptoms (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea, vomiting) alongside common chronic comorbidities like hypertension, diabetes, and GERD without a unifying clinical story. This lacks a specific disease pattern and is not useful for identifying a coherent condition cluster.

phenotypes.label: Foot and ankle overuse disorders
phenotypes.description: Cluster of foot and ankle musculoskeletal conditions, including plantar fasciitis, calcaneal spurs, Achilles and tibialis tendinopathy, pes planus, and degenerative osteoarthritis. Patients present with heel and arch pain, ankle/foot arthralgia, and may have sprains or stress fractures related to biomechanical overload.

phenotypes.label: Retinal and vitreous degenerative disorders
phenotypes.description: Cluster of posterior segment eye disease: vitreous degeneration and opacities, epiretinal membrane, and age-related macular degeneration (dry and exudative), with retinal lattice degeneration, detachment, vascular disorders, and hemorrhage. Co-occurring primary open-angle glaucoma, optic nerve disorders, chorioretinal scarring, dermatochalasis, and dry eye reflect age-related ocular comorbidity in a retina-focused population.

phenotypes.label: Prostate cancer with BPH and ED
phenotypes.description: Male genitourinary cluster centered on prostate cancer with coexisting benign prostatic disease and lower urinary tract symptoms. Features include elevated PSA, BPH with obstruction, prostatitis, urinary frequency/urgency/nocturia, retention or obstruction, and sexual dysfunction including post‑prostatectomy erectile dysfunction and male stress incontinence. This represents men with prostate malignancy accompanied by LUTS and related complications.

phenotypes.label: Ocular rosacea and eye infection
phenotypes.description: Ocular rosacea with associated ocular surface infection, reflecting blepharitis/meibomian gland inflammation and secondary conjunctival involvement. Patients present with chronic eyelid inflammation, redness, irritation, and recurrent infectious flares of the eye.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine very common chronic comorbidities with diabetic kidney disease manifestations that are widespread across many patients in this cohort. This reflects a general baseline pattern rather than a distinctive, targetable clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed renal cancer and psychiatry
phenotypes.description: This grouping blends renal malignancy and kidney/ureter disorders with psychiatric diagnoses including ADHD, generalized anxiety, depression, and OCD. The leading conditions span unrelated organ systems without a single unifying clinical pathway, so this is not a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: PTSD, depression, alcohol/tobacco remission
phenotypes.description: Patients with chronic post‑traumatic stress disorder and recurrent depression, with a documented history of alcohol and tobacco dependence now in remission. Partial edentulism suggests chronic dental disease commonly accompanying long‑standing substance use and psychosocial comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Vascular and pituitary disorders mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions blend peripheral and carotid atherosclerotic disease with claudication alongside pituitary adenomas and hypopituitarism/diabetes insipidus. Additional features such as myasthenia gravis with thymoma, neurofibromatosis, and retinal vein occlusions reflect unrelated mechanisms, so there is no single clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic, cross‑system symptoms such as pain, nausea, chest pain, shortness of breath, vomiting, and diarrhea, with scattered respiratory and constitutional findings. There is no unifying disease process, reflecting a broad, nonspecific presentation rather than a distinct clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are heterogeneous, combining hepatic disease (cirrhosis with portal hypertension/varices, hepatitis C), acute metabolic and electrolyte derangements, sepsis, psychiatric comorbidity, and diabetes complications. There is no single unifying clinical story, so this does not represent a coherent condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: HIV with HPV-related anogenital dysplasia
phenotypes.description: HIV infection predominates with concurrent high‑risk HPV disease and anogenital dysplasia (anal, cervical, vulvar), including carcinoma in situ and condylomata. Kaposi’s sarcoma, viral hepatitis B/C, and other sexually transmitted infections (syphilis, genital herpes) are frequent, consistent with immunosuppression. Serious mental illness (schizophrenia/schizoaffective) and nicotine dependence appear as comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Lung malignancy with COPD and metastases
phenotypes.description: Primary lung malignancy with pulmonary imaging abnormalities and symptoms, including solitary pulmonary nodules, dyspnea, cough, atelectasis, pleural effusion, pneumothorax, and COPD/emphysema. Findings also indicate nodal involvement and distant metastases (brain, bone), with postoperative sequelae such as absence of lung and post-thoracotomy pain.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a mix of broadly encountered dermatologic diagnoses (keratoses, nevi, pigment changes, common skin cancers) that do not define a distinct, actionable phenotype. This cluster reflects general background patterns rather than a focused clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Neurocognitive impairment with falls/fractures
phenotypes.description: Neurocognitive disorders predominate, with amnesia, minimal cognitive impairment, impaired attention, and cognitive‑communication deficits. Co-occurring ataxia, apraxia, traumatic subdural hemorrhage, and multiple fractures—including periprosthetic injury, mechanical complications of implants, and nonunion—suggest fall-related trauma in older adults with underlying cognitive decline.
